title: parthenocissus laetevirens
intro: Parthenocissus laetevirens, also known as the Virginia creeper or woodbine, is a type of flowering plant that is native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Parthenocissus laetevirens include reducing fever, relieving pain, and acting as an anti-cancer agent. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying and crushing the leaves, stems, or roots, and are used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions.
